title: Energizing Argentinian White Yam Empanadas
description: A flavorful empanada filled with a silky white yam and bhuna mixture, perfect for a quick and energizing snack.
instructions: In a large pan, heat 1/4 cup of lard over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the white yam, bhuna paste, cumin, and paprika. Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the yam is slightly tender.
In a separate bowl, combine the flour, salt, and beaten eggs. Mix until a dough forms.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place 1-2 tbsp of the yam mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Heat the remaining 1/4 cup of lard in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Add the empanadas to the pan and cook until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes on each side.
